description: Controlled vocabulary for spatial co-localization analysis in mouse optic nerve. Three distinct experimental conditions are present in `obs.condition`, identified ONLY as `condition_A`, `condition_B`, and `condition_C`. An enrichment ratio > 1.0 indicates spatial co-localization beyond chance, < 1.0 indicates spatial avoidance.

enrichment_category_descriptions.strongly_enriched: Enrichment ratio >= 1.5; cells strongly co-localize beyond chance expectation.
enrichment_category_descriptions.moderately_enriched: Enrichment ratio 1.2–1.5; cells reliably co-localize above chance.
enrichment_category_descriptions.weakly_enriched: Enrichment ratio 1.05–1.2; slight spatial preference barely above random.
enrichment_category_descriptions.near_random: Enrichment ratio 0.9–1.05; spatial distribution indistinguishable from random.
enrichment_category_descriptions.weakly_depleted: Enrichment ratio 0.7–0.9; cells mildly avoid each other spatially.
enrichment_category_descriptions.moderately_depleted: Enrichment ratio 0.4–0.7; cells actively avoid each other.
enrichment_category_descriptions.strongly_depleted: Enrichment ratio < 0.4; cells found in completely separate spatial compartments.

answer_conventions.cell_type_A: Exactly one label from `cell_type_vocabulary_A`. The first cell type in the pair (alphabetically earlier).
answer_conventions.cell_type_B: Exactly one label from `cell_type_vocabulary_B`. The second cell type in the pair (alphabetically later). Must differ from cell_type_A (heterotypic pairs only).
answer_conventions.enrichment_category: Exactly one label from `enrichment_category_vocabulary` describing the magnitude of spatial co-localization for this pair.
answer_conventions.top_k_rule: Return exactly 3 heterotypic pairs with the STRONGEST spatial co-localization, ordered from highest to lowest.
answer_conventions.depleted_rule: Return the single heterotypic pair with the STRONGEST spatial avoidance.
